title: Capillary Exchange, Hemodynamics, and BP Control

questions.text: Which mechanism is the most important method of capillary exchange?
According to Starling's law, which pressure pushes fluid out of the capillaries into the interstitial space?
A patient has low albumin levels due to liver disease. How would this affect Starling's forces?

answers.text: Decreased BCOP, causing increased filtration and edema
Decreased IFHP, causing fluid retention in vessels

questions.text: Mean arterial pressure (MAP) is calculated as:
Which factor does NOT increase vascular resistance?

answers.text: Decreased lumen diameter due to vasoconstriction
Increased total blood vessel length from obesity

questions.text: The skeletal muscle pump and respiratory pump primarily assist with:

answers.text: Venous return of blood to the heart
Oxygenation of blood in the lungs

questions.text: When blood pressure falls, baroreceptors respond by:

answers.text: Sending faster impulses to the cardiovascular center
Sending slower impulses, decreasing parasympathetic response and increasing sympathetic response
Releasing epinephrine from the adrenal medulla

questions.text: Chemoreceptors detect changes in which of the following?
A trauma patient arrives with significant external bleeding. Which type of shock are they most likely experiencing?
A patient with a severe bacterial infection develops widespread vasodilation and low blood pressure. This is most consistent with which type of shock?
